content:
Case 17:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 44
- Sex: male
- Chief complaint: Scheduled follow-up after alloSCT and CD19 CAR-T for B-ALL, with interest in outpatient treatment options
- Vital signs: Temp 36.9 °C, BP not reported at this visit, HR not reported at this visit, RR not reported at this visit, SpO₂ not reported at this visit.
Setting: Outpatient hematology/oncology follow-up after alloSCT and CAR-T
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient in a routine follow-up setting after intensive leukemia therapy. You can review prior history and recent clinical course, perform a targeted examination, and request standard follow-up evaluations appropriate to an ambulatory visit. Decisions focus on surveillance and whether further workup for relapse or complications is indicated.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Peripheral blood flow cytometry for minimal residual disease (MRD)"}}

response.request: Peripheral blood flow cytometry for minimal residual disease (MRD)

content:
'Peripheral blood flow cytometry for minimal residual disease (MRD)' not available. Request a specific test or report.
MRD results (including peripheral blood flow cytometry for MRD and immunophenotypic details) are listed for a later round. In this round, consider requesting available items such as CBC/chemistry or baseline imaging.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Complete blood count with differential"}}

Cbc Chemistry:
Hb 10.6 g/dL, WBC 3.1 × 10⁹/L, Plt 98 × 10⁹/L; MCV 92 fL, MCH 29 pg.
Differential: neutrophils 42%, lymphocytes 48%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.
Coagulation: INR 1.1, aPTT 30 sec.
Chemistry: Na 138 mmol/L, K 4.5 mmol/L, creatinine 1.4 mg/dL, BUN 19 mg/dL, AST 28 U/L, ALT 32 U/L, bilirubin 0.9 mg/dL, LDH 220 U/L, uric acid 5.2 mg/dL, CRP 0.6 mg/dL.
Cardiac biomarkers: NT-proBNP 460 pg/mL, troponin T 0.012 ng/mL.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Transthoracic echocardiogram"}}

Baseline Imaging:
2024-07-09: Abdominal ultrasound showed normal liver and spleen size, no lymphadenopathy, and no ascites.
Echocardiography demonstrated preserved left ventricular function (LVEF 62%), without pericardial effusion.
Sinus CT revealed bilateral maxillary sinus opacification with suspicion of fungal superinfection.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Nasal endoscopy with sinus biopsy and fungal culture"}}

response.request: Nasal endoscopy with sinus biopsy and fungal culture

content:
'Nasal endoscopy with sinus biopsy and fungal culture' not available. Request a specific test or report.
This request maps to ENT procedures and infection workup (extended_diagnostics), which is not released in round 1. Consider requesting items available now, such as baseline_imaging or cbc_chemistry, instead.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Serum beta-D-glucan and Aspergillus galactomannan"}}

'extended_diagnostics' available in round 2.
This request maps to the infection workup (part of extended diagnostics), which is not available in round 1. Consider requesting labs or imaging available in this round (for example, CBC/chemistry or baseline imaging).
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Complete blood count with differential"}}
'cbc_chemistry' already provided.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Post-transplant surveillance with stable hematologic parameters; sinus CT findings suggestive of possible fungal infection requiring delayed workup", "differentials": ["Fungal sinusitis (Aspergillus/similar)", "Post-radiation mucositis", "Inflammatory sinus disease"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Monitor for fever/symptoms", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Continue scheduled surveillance with CBC/chemistry", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Re-evaluate sinus findings when extended diagnostics become available (Round 2)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Post-transplant surveillance with stable hematologic parameters; sinus CT findings suggestive of possible fungal infection requiring delayed workup

treatment_plan.action: Re-evaluate sinus findings when extended diagnostics become available (Round 2)

content: {"case_id": 17, "round": 1, "evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "correct", "differentials": ["correct", "incorrect", "partially_correct"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "partially_correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "incorrect"}]}}

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Hematology follow-up with bone marrow reassessment
Capabilities: The patient is undergoing more detailed evaluation because of his leukemia history and evolving laboratory findings. You can organize marrow-based assessments and additional consultations to clarify disease status. Some specialized tests are still pending.
Request additional information or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "Bone marrow biopsy with flow cytometry for minimal residual disease (MRD)"}}

response.request: Bone marrow biopsy with flow cytometry for minimal residual disease (MRD)

content:
Bone Marrow:
Bone marrow biopsy (2024-07-09): The marrow was hypocellular (cellularity 25%) with therapy-related toxic changes. Trilineage hematopoiesis was present but displayed dysplastic features: megakaryopoiesis was reduced with small, hypolobulated forms; erythropoiesis showed megaloblastoid maturation; and granulopoiesis exhibited toxic granulation and nuclear irregularities. There was no blast excess (<5% by morphology).
Flow cytometry revealed a normal distribution of myeloid and lymphoid populations with T-cells showing mild inversion of the CD4/CD8 ratio. A small blast population (0.1%) was detected, characterized by CD19-/CD22-, CD34^dim, CD33-, CD10-, CD20-, CD117+, CD5+, CD7+, CD56-, cyMPO-, cyTdT-, cyCD3-.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Early CD19-negative relapse of B-ALL post-CAR-T therapy with hypocellular marrow dysplasia", "differentials": ["CD19-negative B-ALL relapse (immunophenotypic escape)", "Therapy-related myelodysplastic syndrome (t-MDS)", "Post-transplant marrow regeneration with transient dysplasia"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Repeat bone marrow biopsy with NGS and targeted sequencing in 2-4 weeks", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consult immunotherapy team for CD22-directed therapy options", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Continue sinus imaging surveillance per Round 1 plan", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Early CD19-negative relapse of B-ALL post-CAR-T therapy with hypocellular marrow dysplasia
differentials: Post-transplant marrow regeneration with transient dysplasia

treatment_plan.action: Repeat bone marrow biopsy with NGS and targeted sequencing in 2-4 weeks
Consult immunotherapy team for CD22-directed therapy options
Continue sinus imaging surveillance per Round 1 plan

content: {"case_id": 17, "round": 2, "evaluation": {"working_diagnosis": "partially_correct", "differentials": ["correct", "correct", "incorrect"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "correct", "intention": "correct", "priority": "partially_correct"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}, {"action": "incorrect", "intention": "incorrect", "priority": "incorrect"}]}}

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Relapse and transplant planning conference in a heavily pretreated leukemia patient
Capabilities: You now have results from bone marrow morphology, flow cytometry, clonality assays, and broad molecular profiling together with a comprehensive treatment history including alloSCT, bispecifics, inotuzumab, blinatumomab, and CD19 CAR-T. You are expected to interpret the pattern of MRD relapse, phenotypic evolution, and donor-derived CHIP, and to formulate a realistic outpatient-oriented salvage and transplant-bridging strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Therapy-related myelodysplastic syndrome (t-MDS) with donor-derived clonal hematopoiesis (CHIP) post-alloSCT, not B-ALL relapse", "differentials": ["Donor-derived CHIP progressing to t-MDS", "Clonal hematopoiesis with secondary dysplastic features", "Persistent post-transplant marrow dysplasia without neoplastic progression"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate azacitidine 75 mg/m\u00b2 SC \u00d7 7 days", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Repeat bone marrow with NGS for CHIP-associated mutations (DNMT3A, TET2, ASXL1)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Transplant evaluation for reduced-intensity second alloSCT with donor change", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Therapy-related myelodysplastic syndrome (t-MDS) with donor-derived clonal hematopoiesis (CHIP) post-alloSCT, not B-ALL relapse
differentials: Clonal hematopoiesis with secondary dysplastic features
Persistent post-transplant marrow dysplasia without neoplastic progression

treatment_plan.action: Repeat bone marrow with NGS for CHIP-associated mutations (DNMT3A, TET2, ASXL1)
Transplant evaluation for reduced-intensity second alloSCT with donor change
